Scenario.title: APT28 Nearest Neighbor Campaign Targets Healthcare Systems via Spearphishing
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple APT28 leverages spearphishing and ransomware to disrupt healthcare operations, exploiting basic security gaps in medical institutions.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 511: Infiltration of Software Development Environment
stage.description: Attackers send malicious PDF/Word documents伪装成urgent patient data updates via email. Recipients opening the attachment triggers exploitation of CVE-2023-32049, embedding malware in the healthcare organization's network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 662: Adversary in the Browser (AiTB)
stage.description: Embedded macros or exploits (e.g., CVE-2018-0167) within the phishing document execute payloads automatically when enabled, bypassing need for further user interaction to establish foothold.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Ransomware encrypts critical patient records and hospital systems, locking down operations and demanding cryptocurrency payments to restore access.

Scenario.summary: APT28 exploited spearphishing and known vulnerabilities to deploy ransomware against healthcare targets, leveraging basic techniques to maximize operational disruption.
